title: Income Tax, Simply
summary: Tax is why your take-home is less than your CTC. You don't need to be an expert — just understand the slab idea, the difference between the old and new regimes, and that you should never spend only to 'save tax'.

sections.heading: Slabs, not a single rate
sections.body: Income tax is charged in slabs: different portions of your income are taxed at different rates. So earning a bit more never leaves you with less in hand — only the extra slice is taxed at the higher rate.

sections.heading: Two regimes
sections.body: India has an old regime (more deductions, e.g. for certain investments and rent) and a new regime (lower rates, fewer deductions). Which is better depends on your situation; many use a simple calculator to compare.

sections.heading: Don't let the tail wag the dog
sections.body: Some investments give tax benefits, which is a nice bonus. But spending or locking money only to save tax often costs you more than it saves. Choose good financial decisions first; let tax breaks be a side benefit.

quiz.prompt: Because income tax uses slabs, earning a little more:
options: Can leave you with less money overall
Only taxes the extra portion at the higher rate
Taxes all your income at the top rate

quiz.prompt: A sensible attitude to tax-saving investments is:
options: Pick good decisions first; treat tax breaks as a bonus

quiz.prompt: Compared with the old regime, India's new tax regime generally offers:
Why is your take-home pay lower than your CTC?
options: Because tax and deductions are taken out
Because CTC is paid only once a year

quiz.prompt: Which regime is 'better' for a given person?
options: The old regime is always better
The new regime is always better
It depends on their situation; a calculator helps compare

disclaimer: Educational content only — a simplified overview, not tax advice. Rules change; check current law or a professional.
